howto.title: The Art of Writing Manga Chapters
howto.intro: Welcome to the writer's guide! This page will walk you through the key elements of crafting compelling stories for your manga.
howto.plotweaversText: PlotWeavers is an innovative web application that enables manga fans to collaborate on creating interactive stories. The name ‘Weavers’ symbolises the interweaving of stories by the community.
howto.mangaPlotText1: A manga isn't just a drawing; it's a complete narrative with a plot, characters, and a world. While a manga is the visual art, the plot is the story's text, structure, and emotional core.
howto.mangaPlotText2: Most chapters follow a linear structure with a clear beginning, middle, and end. However, some stories use a branching structure with multiple perspectives or timelines. Always guide your readers through the story and build tension with a compelling story arc for each chapter.
howto.genresText: Before you start, it's helpful to know the different types of stories you can tell.
howto.genreShonen: Adventure, action, aimed at young males.
howto.genreShojo: Romance, drama, aimed at young females.
howto.genreSeinen: Complex themes, aimed at adult males.
howto.genreIsekai: A protagonist is transported to another world.
howto.tropesText: Tropes are common narrative patterns. Examples include: a hero with a dark past, a sudden transformation, a rivalry between characters, or a Tsundere (a character who is initially cold but reveals a warmer side).
howto.chapterHook: A captivating opening that grabs attention.
howto.chapterCliffhanger: End on high tension for the next chapter.
howto.chapterDialogue: Reveal character and mood with dialogue & description.
howto.chapterFormatting: Keep text readable with clear formatting.
howto.rulesViolence: Violence may be shown but not celebrated.
howto.rulesHate: Zero tolerance for racism, discrimination, or hate.
howto.rulesErotica: Erotic themes permitted, but not explicit content.
howto.tipsFeedback: Community feedback is gold for improvement.
howto.inspirationText: To get inspired, check out popular manga and anime to study their storytelling techniques. Even the best writers take inspiration from others!
